journal.name: Vanderbilt Journal of Entertainment and Technology Law

registry_refs.key: Vanderbilt Journal of Entertainment and Technology Law
registry_refs.notes: W&L rank 139; registry id 642 has both legacy WordPress and Digital Commons rows.

publication_scope.notes: WordPress is the preferred current archive and metadata layer; article landings and files hand off to scholarship.law.vanderbilt.edu/jetlaw/.

evidence.notes: Legacy apex robots route returned a generic HTML 404; no allow/disallow decision can be inferred.
Only /wp-admin/ is disallowed; public JETLaw pages are allowed.
Publication and viewcontent routes are not disallowed, but /do/ is disallowed and must never be used.
Requested legacy /jetlaw/past-volumes/ redirected to the new WordPress overview, which links current Volume 28 works to Digital Commons.
WordPress archive exposes current accordion records with volume, issue, title, date, author, type, abstract, and Digital Commons article links; older issue cards hand off to issue landings.
Digital Commons cover page exposes h1, .author, og:description, bepress_citation_title/author/date/volume/issue/pdf_url, and recommended citation.
One bounded GET with article Referer returned the shared 744-byte Digital Commons denial HTML, not PDF; probing stopped immediately.

reconnaissance.notes: The requested legacy WordPress route has migrated. Discovery and rich metadata pass through current WordPress plus Digital Commons article pages; file delivery is blocked by the shared Digital Commons 403 gate.

include: JETLaw print archive and article records
Digital Commons jetlaw article pages and article PDFs
scope.notes: Require /publications/jetlaw/ on WordPress or /jetlaw/ plus context=jetlaw on Digital Commons.

edges.notes: Read More and Download PDF labels both point to cover pages, not raw files.
First file attempt returned HTTP 403 HTML.

archive_patterns.notes: Numeric volume, issue, and article ordinal.
Currently returns Digital Commons 403 to ordinary GET.

pagination.termination: Single observed page ends at Volume 25 Issue 3 and links the full Digital Commons archive for older material.
pagination.notes: No pagination control observed; historical coverage requires the scoped handoff.

layout_eras.label: Vanderbilt Law WordPress archive
layout_eras.notes: Current accordions are metadata-rich; older cards are issue-level.

layout_eras.label: Digital Commons article repository
layout_eras.notes: Article metadata is reachable; file GET is denied.

sources.example: Turning the Tide: Reforming Western Water Law in the Age of AI Infrastructure
sources.notes: WordPress accordion title is a fallback.

authors.missingness: Present in sampled work; front matter may lack authors.

sources.example: The rise of generative artificial intelligence (AI) has fueled a global race...
sources.notes: WordPress accordion contains the same long-form abstract.

pages.missingness: First page present in sample; end page unknown.

sources.example: Rhyma Asim, Turning the Tide..., 28 Vanderbilt Journal of Entertainment and Technology Law 795 (2026)

patterns.notes: Ordinary GET with Referer returned 403 HTML; a browser/cookie-capable path may be required.

validation.notes: Response was confirmed not to be PDF; no article file was validated.

barriers.notes: Digital Commons viewcontent returned 403 HTML even with article Referer.
Legacy vanderbilt.edu/jetlaw paths redirect to the new law.vanderbilt.edu publication overview.

access.politeness_notes: Robots checked on each host; one WordPress archive, one cover page, and one failed file smoke only; stopped on first 403.

verdict.summary: The new Vanderbilt Law WordPress archive is the best metadata seed and hands off deterministically to rich Digital Commons article pages, but the representative viewcontent PDF returned HTTP 403 HTML.
blockers: Digital Commons article PDF delivery is blocked by a 403 gate
Older than Volume 25 Issue 3 requires a historical archive handoff
Only one article cover page was sampled
verdict.next_action: Implement metadata-only WordPress-to-Digital-Commons enumeration and test an approved browser/cookie-aware file lane without using /do/.
verdict.implementation_notes: Seed /publications/jetlaw/print-archive/, parse current accordions, treat all scholarship Read More/Download PDF links as cover pages, use bepress_citation_* metadata, and stop file work on first 403.
